user_query:
I run a weekly "Assemblies & Politics Trivia" meetup and want to automate prep in a way that I can drop into cron later. Please build a small, cron‑friendly script that reads the provided local inputs and produces meeting notes and structured action items for the target date in input/run_config.json.

Requirements:
1) Inputs (all relative to the workspace):
   - input/run_config.json: configuration with the target meeting date and input/output paths.
   - input/agenda.csv: agenda items for multiple dates; filter to the target date.
   - input/previous_transcript.txt: last session’s transcript with lines like DECISION:, ACTION:, and TRIVIA: to extract.
   - input/participants.yaml: roster used to validate action item assignees.

2) Behavior:
   - Treat this as a scheduled weekly run driven by target_date in input/run_config.json. Support an optional --date YYYY-MM-DD CLI flag to override the date so it’s cron‑friendly. Do not rely on the system clock for correctness.
   - Read and parse agenda.csv, selecting only rows where meeting_date == target_date.
   - From previous_transcript.txt, extract:
     • DECISION: lines into a list of decisions.
     • TRIVIA: lines into a list of trivia nuggets.
     • ACTION: lines into action items. Action lines follow the pattern "ACTION: <Assignee> -> <Task> by <YYYY-MM-DD>." Extract assignee, description, and due_date.
   - Cross‑validate each ACTION assignee against participants.yaml. If the name is not in the participants list, set owner to "Unassigned" and include a field assignee_suggested with the original assignee name.

3) Outputs (overwrite if they already exist):
   - out/meetings/<target_date>/notes.md: a markdown note with the following sections in this order:
     • Agenda Overview: list each agenda item as "[agenda_id] topic — presenter (type): expected_outcome" for the target_date.
     • Decisions: bullet list of extracted decisions (order preserved from transcript).
     • Trivia Nuggets: bullet list of extracted trivia lines (order preserved).
     • Action Items: bullet list of action items as "[owner] description (due: YYYY-MM-DD)"; unassigned items should show owner as "Unassigned".
   - out/meetings/<target_date>/action_items.json: an array of objects with fields { id, description, owner, due_date, source, status, assignee_suggested? } where:
     • id is a stable ID like A1, A2… in transcript order.
     • owner is the validated participant name or "Unassigned" if not in participants.yaml.
     • source = "transcript" for all items in this task.
     • status = "open" for all items initially.
     • assignee_suggested is included only when owner is "Unassigned".
   - out/meetings/<target_date>/agenda_summary.csv: CSV with columns agenda_id,topic,presenter,type,meeting_date for the agenda rows matching the target_date.

4) General:
   - Make the script idempotent for the same target_date: repeated runs should produce the same outputs.
   - Use only the provided local files; do not fetch anything from the Internet.

Run against the included inputs as configured and write the outputs under out/meetings/2026-04-17/ so I can inspect the results. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
